status.headline: Official vote counts, legal DPR threshold applied
status.officialInputs: Vote counts and district-party slate inputs come from prepared source tables in this repo.
status.estimatedOutputs: DPR seat and winner views are generated in Python with the 4% national DPR threshold applied. They are not official certified KPU results.
status.thresholdRule: For DPR 2024, parties below 4% of valid national DPR votes are excluded from DPR seat allocation in every dapil. This does not apply to DPRD.
status.caution: Vote sections still show all parties. Seat sections show the legal threshold-adjusted DPR model.
status.seatMethod: Sainte-Lague highest quotient with explicit dapil seat counts, after filtering to threshold-passing DPR parties, then highest-vote candidates inside winning party slates.

coverageNotes: DPD source coverage currently excludes Papua Barat Daya. Treat this as expected source coverage, not a normalization or join failure.
DPRD kabupaten/kota coverage currently excludes DKI Jakarta in this dataset. Treat this as expected source coverage.

methodology.title: Official Inputs, Estimated Outputs
methodology.body: Candidate votes, party-only votes, provinces, districts, and party identities come from the prepared election tables in this repo. The dashboard then estimates district-party seats and likely winners in Python. Those estimated seats and winner lists are analytical outputs, not official KPU certification.

methodology.title: 4% National DPR Threshold
methodology.body: For DPR 2024, parties below 4% of valid national DPR votes are excluded from DPR seat allocation in every dapil. Vote totals and candidate tables still show all parties, but DPR seat and winner views apply the legal threshold-adjusted model.

methodology.title: What Counts As Party Votes
methodology.body: Party votes are the party-only ballots recorded for a district-party slate. Candidate votes are the personal votes won by individual names on that same slate. Party vote share is the party-only portion of total visible support for the slate.

methodology.title: How Estimated Winners Are Produced
methodology.body: Seat estimates are allocated with the Sainte-Lague highest-quotient method using explicit dapil seat counts. Once a party is estimated to win seats in a district, the highest-vote candidates inside that party's slate are treated as the likely winners.

methodology.title: How To Read District Labels
methodology.body: A party-led district means party-only ballots carry an unusually large share of support. A candidate-led district means support is concentrated more heavily in standout personal vote-getters. Mixed means both effects matter.

methodology.title: Expected Coverage Gaps
methodology.body: Some source gaps are expected and documented rather than treated as failures. In the current source set, Papua Barat Daya is absent from the DPD file, and DKI Jakarta is absent from the DPRD kabupaten/kota file.

glossary.definition: An electoral district. DPR seats are allocated within each dapil, not at the national total alone.
A vote cast for the party slate rather than for a named candidate on that slate.
A personal vote won by an individual candidate within a party slate.
A district where party-only ballots make up a relatively large share of support and no single candidate dominates as strongly.
A district where personal vote-getters dominate more strongly and party-only ballots play a smaller role.
A candidate treated as a likely winner by the Python seat-allocation model. This is not an official certified winner list.
For DPR 2024, parties below 4% of valid national DPR votes are excluded from DPR seat allocation. This national rule does not apply to DPRD.
